0.description: Aldo Torfell spoke Prophecy of the Broken Seat at Marara, a prophecy of fall tied to Rain Oath of Elhelm and Elport the Gale Keeper, naming Marara as its sign through through thunder breaks, wind warnings, and a thunder mark appearing without hands.

1.description: Nash Kelton spoke Prophecy of the Broken Seat at Marara, a prophecy of fall tied to Rain Oath of Elhelm and Harton the Thunder Lord, naming Marara as its sign through through thunder breaks, wind warnings, and a broken cloud appearing without hands.

2.description: Gorm Wendhold spoke Prophecy of the Rain Door at Marara, a prophecy of storm tied to Rain Oath of Elhelm and Quaovar the Rain Mother, naming Marara as its sign through through thunder breaks, wind warnings, and a rain drum appearing without hands.

3.description: Bram Valfield the 5th spoke Prophecy of the Return at Peldun, a prophecy of journey tied to Counting Creed of Galdrim and Galstead the Market Keeper, naming Peldun as its sign through through ledgers, road news, market bells, and a road coin appearing without hands.

4.description: Gorm Pelhelm the 2nd spoke Prophecy of the Bright Token at Peldun, a prophecy of artifact tied to Counting Creed of Galdrim and Marara the Scale Mother, naming Peldun as its sign through through ledgers, road news, market bells, and a road coin appearing without hands.

5.description: Gorm Pelhelm spoke Prophecy of the Named Work at Peldun, a prophecy of artifact tied to Counting Creed of Galdrim and Corvale the Coin Witness, naming Peldun as its sign through through ledgers, road news, market bells, and a market bell appearing without hands.

6.description: Sorn Orhelm spoke Prophecy of the Crowned Day at Nordrim, a prophecy of rise tied to Counting Creed of Kelwatch and Ordrim the Coin Witness, naming Sorn Orhelm as its sign through through ledgers, road news, market bells, and a market bell appearing without hands.

7.description: Quill Ingborn the 4th spoke Prophecy of the Long Road at Nordrim, a prophecy of journey tied to Counting Creed of Kelwatch and Lorara the Scale Mother, naming Nordrim as its sign through through ledgers, road news, market bells, and a balanced scale appearing without hands.

8.description: Gala Vorhill the 5th spoke Prophecy of the Far Step at Nordrim, a prophecy of journey tied to Counting Creed of Kelwatch and Rivport the Road Father, naming Nordrim as its sign through through ledgers, road news, market bells, and a sealed ledger appearing without hands.

9.description: Odo Lorwatch the 6th spoke Prophecy of the Clashing Dawn at Junwatch, a prophecy of war tied to Peak Rite of Armere and Ulia the Stone Father, naming Junwatch as its sign through through cairn falls, cave echoes, and a white peak appearing without hands.

10.description: Pela Ingbrook the 12th spoke Prophecy of the Bright Token at Junwatch, a prophecy of artifact tied to Peak Rite of Armere and Nordrim the Peak Mother, naming Junwatch as its sign through through cairn falls, cave echoes, and a cairn stone appearing without hands.

11.description: Gala Orhelm the 7th spoke Prophecy of the Bright Token at Junwatch, a prophecy of artifact tied to Peak Rite of Armere and Arton the Deep Keeper, naming Junwatch as its sign through through cairn falls, cave echoes, and a deep cave appearing without hands.

12.description: Gorm Pelia the 8th spoke Prophecy of the High Name at Riveria, a prophecy of rise tied to Night Path of Coreria and Ordrim the Far Seer, naming Gorm Pelia the 8th as its sign through through night charts, falling lights, and a moon bowl appearing without hands.

13.description: Tovin Korddale the 7th spoke Prophecy of the Rising at Riveria, a prophecy of rise tied to Night Path of Coreria and Ilvale the Night Mother, naming Tovin Korddale the 7th as its sign through through night charts, falling lights, and a silver lantern appearing without hands.

14.description: Fiora Noreria the 9th spoke Prophecy of the Long Road at Riveria, a prophecy of journey tied to Night Path of Coreria and Quaovar the Lantern Lord, naming Riveria as its sign through through night charts, falling lights, and a falling star appearing without hands.

15.description: Cira Quilgate the 19th spoke Prophecy of the Rising at Nordrim, a prophecy of rise tied to Counting Creed of Kelwatch and Ordrim the Coin Witness, naming Nordrim copper seal as its sign through through ledgers, road news, market bells, and a market bell appearing without hands.

16.description: Nola Everwatch the 14th spoke Prophecy of the Crossing at Ulstead, a prophecy of journey tied to Counting Creed of Galdrim and Galstead the Market Keeper, naming Dell Rudglen the 13th as its sign through through ledgers, road news, market bells, and a road coin appearing without hands.
